# Configuration

This guide covers setting up `ij` via the `ij init` wizard and working around the `sudo su -` limitation in `shell_commands`.

## Quick Start

```bash
ij init
```

Interactive wizard that creates `~/.config/ij/config.yaml`.

## Generated Config

```yaml
# ij configuration file
# Generated by `ij init`

# (string) AWS profile name
aws_profile: default
# (string) AWS CLI config file path
aws_config_file: ~/.aws/config
# (list<string>) Regions to scan, empty means all regions
scan_regions:
  - ap-northeast-2
# (bool) Only show running instances
running_only: true
# (string) Log level: trace, debug, info, warn, error
log_level: info
# Shell commands executed on SSM connect
shell_commands:
  # (bool) Enable shell commands
  enabled: true
  # (list<string>) Commands to execute, joined with ";"
  commands:
    - "echo $(grep PRETTY_NAME /etc/os-release | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d '\"'), up $(uptime -p | sed 's/up //')"
    - "sudo su -"
```

CLI flags always override config file values. See `ij --help` for all flags.

## Shell Commands and `sudo su -`

Commands are joined with `;` and sent as a single string. `sudo su -` opens a new login shell, so **any command after it is ignored**.

```yaml
# BAD: "cd /var/log" never executes
commands:
  - "sudo su -"
  - "cd /var/log"
```

**Workaround 1** — Place `sudo su -` last (root shell only, no post-login setup):

```yaml
commands:
  - "echo $(grep PRETTY_NAME /etc/os-release | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d '\"'), up $(uptime -p | sed 's/up //')"
  - "sudo su -"
```

**Workaround 2** — Use `sudo -i bash -c` (run commands inside root shell):

```yaml
commands:
  - "sudo -i bash -c 'cd /var/log && exec bash'"
```

`exec bash` keeps the interactive shell open after `cd`.

## Config File Path

1. `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ij/config.yaml`
2. `~/.config/ij/config.yaml` (fallback)
